Meta.title: CureCompass
Meta.description: Trusted condition-focused research updates in simple language.

Nav.briefingsTitle: Plain-language summaries of your followed conditions

Home.headline: Track real progress for the conditions you care about.
Home.sub: Trusted medical research updates, explained in simple language.
Home.tagline: Clear answers. Real progress. Hope for the journey.

Footer.blurb: CureCompass explains trusted research in plain language. It is <bold>not</bold> personal medical advice. Always talk to a qualified clinician about your care.

Profile.title: Profile
Profile.intro: Account and preferences (research information only — not medical advice).
Profile.languageHelp: Use the language control in the header (English / עברית). Hebrew uses right-to-left layout. Research text from sources may stay in English.

Dashboard.title: Your dashboard
Dashboard.digestPreviewBriefingTeaser: A recent research briefing is available. Use “Open research briefings” below for the full text.
Dashboard.digestPreviewNoMajor: No major changes in the last 24 hours.
Dashboard.digestPreviewHasUpdates: You have new trusted updates available.
Dashboard.loadError: Could not load dashboard. Please sign in again.
Dashboard.checkUpdatesBody: Pull the latest from PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and openFDA. When new items are found, we automatically start plain-language summaries (needs OpenAI key on the server). Refresh after a short wait to see them.
Dashboard.scanQueued: Job queued — ensure Redis and a Celery worker are running for background completion.
Dashboard.scanEnrich: Simple-language summaries are generating in the background—refresh in a minute or two for easier explanations.
Dashboard.latestSub: Newest trusted items we've indexed, with source date. Run ingestion if this list is empty.
Dashboard.emptyFollowTitle: Follow a condition to see updates
Dashboard.emptyFollowSummary: Choose a condition to track so we can show the newest trusted research, trials, and regulatory items here with dates.
Dashboard.emptyFollowWhy: A focused feed helps you spot meaningful changes without wading through unrelated news.
Dashboard.emptyIngestSummary: We don't have indexed items for your followed conditions right now. After ingestion runs, the latest updates will appear here with source dates.
Dashboard.emptyIngestWhy: Regular monitoring helps you catch meaningful progress while avoiding information overload.
Dashboard.trialsIntro: Open studies on ClinicalTrials.gov for your followed conditions (newest verified first).
Dashboard.trialsNoFollow: Follow a condition to see recruiting trials here.
Dashboard.trialsEmpty: No recruiting trials in the index yet for your conditions. After ingestion, they will appear here.

Bookmarks.title: Saved updates
Bookmarks.intro: Bookmarks from your research feed. Open an update to remove it from here or use Remove.
Bookmarks.signIn: Please sign in to view bookmarks.
Bookmarks.empty: No bookmarks yet. Use “Save” on an update card on the dashboard or condition page.

Digests.title: Research briefings
Digests.intro: A <bold>briefing</bold> is one easy-to-read write-up of recent trusted research for conditions you follow (not a live list of every article). We build it from the same indexed sources as your dashboard, in plain language. You can create a briefing below anytime; in production, briefings can also be generated on a schedule or emailed when your settings allow.
Digests.signIn: Please sign in to view research briefings.
Digests.createSectionBody: Builds a new briefing right away for all followed conditions, or for one condition if you enter its slug. Your notification settings still apply (e.g. email only if enabled).
Digests.listEmpty: No briefings yet. Use “Create a briefing now” above once you have followed conditions and indexed updates—or wait for an automated job if your server runs one.

Condition.fallbackDescription: Trusted research updates for this condition.
Condition.disclaimer: Educational research tracking only. Not diagnosis or treatment advice.
Condition.updatesIntro: Plain-language summaries from trusted indexed sources. Run ingestion from the dashboard if this list is empty.
Condition.emptyUpdatesSummary: After you follow this condition, an admin or automated job can run ingestion to pull PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and openFDA records.
Condition.emptyUpdatesWhy: Once data is ingested and enriched, updates appear here with evidence badges and summaries.
Condition.trialsIntro: Studies indexed from ClinicalTrials.gov for this condition (newest verified first). Not all are recruiting.
Condition.trialsEmpty: No trials in the index yet. Ingestion will populate trial records for this condition.
Condition.askIntroModes: Choose whether answers draw from indexed research only, your uploaded PDFs for this condition only, or both (kept separate in the model prompt). Educational use only—not a diagnosis.
Condition.docsSectionHint: PDF only, text-based files work best. Processing runs after upload. You can delete a file anytime.
Condition.docsConsentLabel: I understand this upload is stored for my account to power summaries and Ask AI, is not a substitute for professional care, and I authorize processing as described in the app’s privacy materials.
Condition.docsEmpty: No documents yet. Upload a PDF above.
Condition.docsFollowToUpload: Follow this condition to upload documents and use document modes.
Condition.docsDeleteConfirm: Delete “{name}”? This removes the file and extracted text from CureCompass.
Condition.askIntroBefore: Answers use only this condition and items already indexed from trusted feeds (PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, openFDA). If nothing is ingested yet, use
Condition.moreLine1b: — defaults for new follows and per-condition email / digest preferences.
Condition.moreLine2b: — how to use CureCompass safely alongside your clinician.
Condition.moreLine3a: Regulatory and trial updates appear in the
Condition.moreLine3b: tab when indexed as separate items. Open the
Condition.moreLine3c: tab for source links and evidence badges.

Onboarding.title: Follow a condition
Onboarding.intro: Choose what to track. You can change this anytime. This app explains research only — not personal medical advice.
Onboarding.ageHelp: We use common research cutoffs: <under18>under 18</under18> vs <adult>18 and older</adult>. This helps filter updates and trials; it isn't a strict medical or legal definition everywhere.
Onboarding.agePediatricDesc: Under 18 — prioritize child- and teen-focused research and trials.
Onboarding.ageAdultDesc: 18 and older — prioritize adult-focused research and trials.
Onboarding.ageBothDesc: All ages — don't narrow by age unless you change this later.
Onboarding.frequencyHelp: Daily means a scheduled briefing about once per day per condition; with email on and SMTP configured, that can include a daily email. You can switch to weekly, turn briefings off, or disable email later under Notification settings.
